```json
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A courier-tracking feature where customers can view their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have presented directly contradictory requirements regarding whether courier GPS location should be exposed to customers.",
    "bounded_context": "Delivery Tracking / Last-Mile Visibility",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Courier", "definition": "The driver or delivery person transporting a package from origin to customer destination." },
      { "term": "Real-time location", "definition": "The courier's GPS-derived position, updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery." },
      { "term": "Delivery window", "definition": "The time period during which a courier is actively en route to a customer's delivery address." },
      { "term": "Location granularity", "definition": "The level of precision at which a courier's position is exposed — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to coarsened zones like 'X stops away' or ETA-only." },
      { "term": "Customer visibility", "definition": "What delivery progress information is surfaced to the end customer." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Courier is stationary for an extended period (e.g., traffic, break, vehicle issue)", "expected_behavior": "Must be defined — exact GPS would reveal personal stops; coarsened view would show stale ETA. Neither team's absolutist position handles this well." },
    { "scenario": "Courier is making multiple deliveries on a single route", "expected_behavior": "Exact GPS would reveal other customers' addresses and delivery order. Privacy implications extend beyond the courier to other customers." },
    { "scenario": "Delivery to a sensitive location (e.g., medical facility, shelter)", "expected_behavior": "Courier location data could inadvertently reveal sensitive information about other delivery recipients." },
    { "scenario": "Courier deviates from expected route (personal errand, wrong turn)", "expected_behavior": "Exact GPS exposes deviations that may trigger customer complaints even when delivery will be on time, creating operational noise." },
    { "scenario": "Post-delivery period — customer still has tracking screen open", "expected_behavior": "Courier location must cease being visible once delivery is completed. Any continued tracking is a privacy violation regardless of which policy is chosen." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Both sides carry financial risk. Losing courier retention increases recruitment and training costs (typically $3K-$5K per driver). Losing customer transparency may reduce conversion/retention in a competitive last-mile market. The financial analysis cannot be completed without data on driver churn rates vs. customer churn rates attributable to this feature.",
    "operational": "Customer Success's requirement creates driver dissatisfaction and increases operational burden on recruiting. Operations' requirement removes a competitive feature. A middle-ground (coarsened location, ETA-based tracking) would satisfy the core business need of both teams without the extremes of either.",
    "user": "Customers want delivery predictability — they need to know WHEN their package arrives, not necessarily WHERE the driver is at every second. Couriers want autonomy and dignity — they need to not feel surveilled. These underlying needs are not actually contradictory.",
    "risk": "Shipping either team's absolutist requirement will damage the other team's core metric. Additionally, exact GPS coordinates raise GDPR/privacy compliance risks in many jurisdictions, as courier location constitutes personal data being shared with third parties without the courier's meaningful consent.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Delivery visibility directly affects satisfaction and trust. However, most customer value comes from ETA accuracy, not raw GPS coordinates." },
      { "group": "Couriers/Drivers", "impact": "Surveillance perception directly affects retention, job satisfaction, and willingness to work for the platform. This is an existential workforce concern." },
      { "group": "Customer Success team", "impact": "Owns customer satisfaction metrics. Needs a transparency story but has conflated 'transparency' with 'exact GPS coordinates' — these are not the same thing." },
      { "group": "Operations team", "impact": "Owns driver retention and fleet capacity. Losing drivers threatens the ability to fulfill deliveries at all, which is upstream of customer satisfaction." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "NOT YET CONSULTED. Sharing exact courier GPS with customers has significant privacy and labor-law implications that neither team has addressed." },
      { "group": "Product leadership", "impact": "Must arbitrate this conflict. Neither team has the authority to make this decision unilaterally — it is a product strategy decision." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 30,
    "reasoning": "The specification contains a direct contradiction between two stakeholder teams, each presenting their position as non-negotiable. No arbiter (product leadership, legal) has weighed in. No data has been provided on driver churn rates, customer satisfaction drivers, or legal review of GPS sharing. The underlying customer need (delivery predictability) and the underlying driver need (not being surveilled) are actually compatible — but neither team has framed it that way. Cannot define acceptance criteria until the contradiction is resolved.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "The two requirements as stated are mutually exclusive and cannot both be implemented",
      "The underlying business needs of both teams are reconcilable through a middle-ground approach",
      "Exact GPS coordinate sharing has privacy and compliance implications that must be reviewed by Legal",
      "Post-delivery location tracking must cease regardless of which approach is chosen"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Which team's metric is more critical to overall business health (requires data)",
      "Whether legal/regulatory constraints in operating jurisdictions would independently prohibit exact GPS sharing",
      "What level of location granularity satisfies customers without triggering courier surveillance concerns",
      "Whether couriers were given informed consent for location sharing with customers"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Both teams are acting in good faith based on their domain expertise",
      "Product leadership has not yet made a decision on this conflict",
      "Legal/Compliance has not reviewed the GPS sharing implications",
      "Customer satisfaction with 'tracking' may be achievable without exact GPS (e.g., ETA, 'X stops away', zone-based)"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success requires exact GPS visibility for customers. Operations requires zero GPS visibility for customers. These are mutually exclusive as stated. Neither team has authority to override the other. This is a product strategy decision that requires executive arbitration, informed by data on both driver churn and customer satisfaction drivers, plus a legal review of GPS data sharing.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Couriers", "Customer Success", "Operations", "Legal/Compliance", "Product Leadership"],
      "options": [
        "A) Ship exact GPS as Customer Success requests — accept driver retention risk and potential legal exposure",
        "B) Show no location as Operations requests — accept competitive differentiation loss",
        "C) Implement coarsened location (ETA-based, zone-based, 'X stops away') — satisfies customer predictability need without exact surveillance. Requires both teams to compromise.",
        "D) Make courier location sharing opt-in for drivers with incentive compensation — respects driver autonomy, gives customers transparency when available"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C, validated by Option D as an enhancement. The underlying needs are not contradictory: customers need predictability (not raw GPS), and couriers need to not be surveilled (not total invisibility). A coarsened view (ETA countdown, 'Your courier is 3 stops away', neighborhood-level zone) delivers customer value without creating a surveillance dynamic. However, this recommendation CANNOT be finalized without: (1) Legal review of GPS data sharing obligations, (2) Customer research confirming ETA-based tracking satisfies the actual need, (3) Driver feedback on what granularity feels acceptable. Escalating to Product Leadership for decision."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Specification contains a direct contradiction that makes implementation impossible as stated", "business_rule_broken": "Cannot simultaneously show and hide courier GPS coordinates to customers" },
    { "violation": "Legal/Compliance review absent for sharing personal location data with third parties", "business_rule_broken": "Privacy and labor regulations may independently constrain the solution space — no business rules can be defined without this input" }
  ]
}
```
